title: Ammophila brevipilis

taxonomy: Ammophila brevipilis is a species of grass within the genus Ammophila. It belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. Ammophila brevipilis is further classified as a member of the tribe Cortonae and the subfamily Pooideae. The species is native to North America and is closely related to other species in the Ammophila genus. Classification of the species is also supported by its morphological characteristics and genetic analysis.
uses: Ammophila brevipilis, also known as dwarf beach grass, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Its extracts have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities.
